# BUG: partial regex match across an `iota` const block silently breaks
# both halves. The moved subset lands in the destination with iota
# resetting from 0 (changing semantic values); the surviving subset in
# the source stays with the anchor, but if the anchor itself was moved
# the source siblings have no initialiser at all (build red).
#
# Hit while splitting internal/infer/infer.go: regex matched only some
# of the ErrorKindXxx specs; destination consts got iota-renumbered and
# source stranded the rest.
#
# Expected fix: refuse partial moves of grouped iota'd const blocks
# unless every spec in the block matches, OR emit a single warning to
# stderr and a structured `bail` exit code so the operator can split
# differently. The status-quo silent corruption is the worst behaviour.

exec sflit -source big.go -sink small.go -regex '^Kind(A|B)$' -move
cmp big.go expected_big.go
cmp small.go expected_small.go

-- big.go --
package p

type Kind int

const (
	KindUnknown Kind = iota
	KindA
	KindB
	KindC
	KindD
)
-- expected_big.go --
package p

type Kind int

const (
	KindUnknown Kind = iota
	_
	_
	KindC
	KindD
)
-- expected_small.go --
package p

const (
	KindA Kind = 1
	KindB Kind = 2
)
